title: Selliguea caudiformis
intro: Selliguea caudiformis, also known as the tail fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. The tail fern has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the tail fern is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
